serving.natural_language_portion: A typical ala carte serving of fiery rotisserie chicken is approximately 200g, representing roughly half a small chicken or a generous portion of mixed cuts.

nutrient_source.title: Fiery Rotisserie Chicken — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Fiery Rotisserie Chicken Ala Carte (cr_id G00010421). All per-100g macros and key micronutrients are taken verbatim from the HPB record with confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Mixed Protein-Fat Foods — Analogous Estimate Based on University of Sydney GI Database; plain roasted/grilled chicken and high-protein low-carbohydrate meat dishes
gi_source.source_match_notes: Carbohydrate content is 0.6g/100g (negligible). No direct GI measurement exists for fiery rotisserie chicken. An analogous GI of 40 (low category) is assigned based on University of Sydney GI Database data for high-protein, low-carbohydrate cooked chicken and meat dishes, which consistently fall in the low GI range due to minimal digestible carbohydrate. Glycemic load per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (40 × 1.2g) / 100 = 0.48, rounded to 0.5. The practical glycaemic impact of this dish is negligible.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium at 579mg/100g is high; a 200g serving delivers approximately 1158mg sodium, representing 58% of the 2000mg daily limit.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to 100g or less, request reduced-salt and reduced-chilli-paste preparation, and avoid additional dipping sauces to keep sodium intake within safe daily limits.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate is negligible at 0.6g/100g and sugar is 0.6g/100g; high protein at 23.4g/100g supports blood glucose stability.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Fiery rotisserie chicken is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its very low carbohydrate content; pair with non-starchy vegetables rather than white rice to maintain a low overall glycaemic load, and monitor portion size given the high fat content of 18g/100g.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Total fat is 18g/100g with estimated saturated fat of approximately 5g/100g; sodium at 579mg/100g also poses cardiovascular risk.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size, remove skin to reduce saturated fat intake, request reduced-salt preparation to lower sodium, and balance the meal with high-fibre vegetables to offset the absence of dietary fibre in this dish.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Nightshade ingredients (chilli, paprika) may aggravate joint symptoms in sensitive individuals; sodium at 579mg/100g may worsen tissue swelling.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis who are sensitive to nightshades should monitor joint symptoms after consumption; request reduced-salt preparation and limit skin consumption to reduce saturated fat intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fiery Rotisserie Chicken Ala Carte is a spicy roasted chicken dish available as a standalone serving in Singapore's Western-style casual dining and fast-food rotisserie outlets. The chicken is marinated in a bold blend of chilli paste, hot sauce, garlic, paprika, rosemary, and thyme before being slow-roasted on a rotisserie spit, producing juicy, tender meat with a deeply caramelised, fiery exterior. The preparation method avoids deep-frying, which limits advanced glycation end-product (AGE) formation compared to fried chicken alternatives. Nutritionally, the dish is high in protein at 23.4g/100g and moderate-to-high in fat at 18g/100g, with negligible carbohydrate at 0.6g/100g, making it suitable for low-carbohydrate dietary patterns. Sodium is elevated at 579mg/100g due to the marinade, which is a key concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. Cholesterol is 129mg/100g, relevant for cardiovascular risk management. The anti-inflammatory spice profile — garlic, paprika, rosemary, thyme — provides polyphenol benefit. A typical ala carte serving is approximately 200g, delivering around 514 kcal, 46.8g protein, 36g fat, and 1158mg sodium. This dish is popular as a lunch or dinner option in Singapore and is commonly served with coleslaw, corn, or bread rolls.
common_user_queries: How much sodium is in rotisserie chicken?
Can diabetics eat spicy rotisserie chicken?
Is rotisserie chicken good for weight loss?
Is rotisserie chicken high in fat?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010421) with a recorded confidence of 0.95. The source is an approved primary regional source for Singapore dishes. All core macros and key micronutrients are present and internally consistent.
notes: Saturated fat is estimated at approximately 5.0g/100g based on USDA FoodData Central data for roasted chicken with skin (USDA SR Legacy Item 05013), as the HPB record does not disaggregate fat fractions. Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, full vitamin panel, phosphorus, zinc, selenium, and moisture data are not reported in the HPB Foodo entry for this dish and are not available in a directly matching approved-source record as of 2026-04-16. Dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per the HPB source record (0.0g/100g). Glycemic index is set to 40 (low category) as an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database data for high-protein, low-carbohydrate cooked chicken and meat dishes; carbohydrate content is negligible at 0.6g/100g and the practical glycaemic impact is minimal. Glycemic load per 200g serving = 0.5. Halal status is assumed based on the Singapore context and rotisserie chicken preparation norms; formal halal certification should be verified with the specific outlet. Gluten-free status is assumed based on the absence of wheat-containing ingredients in the marinade profile; cross-contamination risk at the outlet is not assessed.
